description: Primary-sourced design-token rules — the W3C Design Tokens Community Group format + tiered token architecture. Re-derived from the DTCG spec and the primitive/semantic/component tiering it enables.

rules.title: Tokens follow the DTCG JSON format
rules.rule: A design token is a JSON object with $value and $type; groups nest; $description documents intent. Use the standard format so tooling (Style Dictionary, Figma) interoperates.

rules.title: Three token tiers: primitive → semantic → component
rules.rule: Primitives (raw values: blue-500) feed semantic/alias tokens (color-action, color-bg) which feed component tokens (button-bg). UI references SEMANTIC tokens, never raw primitives — so theming/rebrand changes one layer.

rules.title: Theme by re-pointing alias tokens
rules.rule: Light/dark and brand themes swap the SEMANTIC layer's values (or its primitive references); component and primitive layers stay constant. Drives a single source of truth for every mode.

rules.title: Consistent, intent-based token names
rules.rule: Name semantic tokens by ROLE/intent (color-text-muted, space-inset-sm), not by appearance (color-grey-2). Names survive a visual rebrand; appearance names don't.
rules.dont: color-light-blue (breaks when the brand changes hue).

rules.title: Tokenize the whole system, not just color
rules.rule: Define tokens for the full scale: color, dimension (spacing/size), typography (family/size/weight/line-height), border, shadow, duration, cubic-bezier. Ad-hoc CSS values outside the token set are drift.
